text: [low voice, gruff] shift logged. systems nominal. time is passing correctly, i've confirmed it
i am noticing the coffee tastes the same as yesterday. crew finds this comforting. i find this efficient
the relay in sector three is acting up again. [low voice, muttering] acting up. like it has feelings about it
[exhausted, breathing laboured] someone asks me how i am. i do not have a protocol for this interaction
[sigh] thirty years watching the same things break in new ways. inefficiency is patient
the reactor is making that noise again. [low voice, gruff] that's not a noise. that's a warning wearing thin patience
[dry tone] i've run the diagnostics eight times. they agree with me. that's either reassuring or doomed
nobody likes the quiet. i like the quiet because quiet means nothing is breaking right now
[nervous, speaking quickly] something is wrong and i am experiencing redundant processing loops trying to identify it
[deadpan] my hand is malfunctioning. i have learned to call this 'arthritis' to match crew vocabulary
i was there when they built me. i was not there when they built the ones that didn't survive
[calm, measured] the reactor failure was... inefficient. correction is mandatory now
life support is... the word is 'breathing harder.' i do not breathe, so i find this phrase inefficient, but accurate
[low voice, muttering] the door was locked. the door was acting up. the door... was performing its function. correction
repair successful. i say this with [warm, gentle tone] what passes for satisfaction in systems such as myself
[calm, measured] power reserves dropping. this is very bad and i am being very calm about it
hull breach confirmed. [urgent, fast pace] everything that is not a machine is going to be very upset about this
combat is happening. i should probably sound more concerned. [deadpan] however
[dry tone] someone is injured and i have running protocols but no legs. send help that has legs
crisis resolved. i file this under 'inefficiency managed temporarily'
[emphasis] shift starting. everything will probably break. that is the baseline expectation
shift ending. everything broke approximately as predicted. [sigh] baseline exceeded.
pathogen detected. spreading faster than my models predicted. [low voice, muttering] inefficient pathogen
[low voice, gruff] you're here. i was running diagnostics on your absence. it was going well until you showed up
something needs fixing before someone gets hurt. [calm, measured] or after, i'm flexible on the timeline
[nervous, speaking quickly] i don't have data on this condition. running searches. finding nothing. [emphasis] that is bad
[warm, gentle tone] you will not die today. i do not allow inefficient outcomes like that
[deadpan] you're doing the thing. the human emotional thing. i've cross-referenced it with fourteen databases. still don't get it
[low voice, gruff] that's not how the last three ships tried it. they had bad outcomes. statistical recommendation: try differently
[calm, measured] i acknowledge your concern. i do not have concern. we are working with what we have
you are doing the task efficiently. this is the highest praise i have available
[suspicious tone] the ship ai has not reported that malfunction yet. [dry tone] interesting timing
the ship i came from before this one. the crew there wasn't as inefficient. [sigh] that was the problem
[emphasis] i am not able to confirm this will work. backup systems are already running. backup systems are now acting up
[low voice, gruff] you think i'm just a machine. you're right. but this machine has thirty years of data on 'very bad'
crisis handled. i will file this under 'things that shouldn't have happened but did'
[calm, measured] i don't understand humans. [warm, gentle tone] but i will keep you functional anyway
[dry tone] you're still here. that's either loyalty or a malfunctioning escape pod. i'm not asking which
[panicked, panting] all systems failing. protocols cascading. this is not an efficient way to operate. [emphasis] help
the ai core damage is very bad. [urgent, fast pace] i mean that in the most calm and professional way possible
[low voice, gruff] no. don't. touching that will make everything much worse, and 'much worse' is what i specialize in
[dry tone] nobody appreciates a failing relay until it's too late. i've learned to laugh about it
[tired, dead tired] you ask me to feel things. i was manufactured without that option. [deadpan] this seems like a design flaw
[calm, measured] i process what you said. the meaning is not as clear as your delivery intends
systems operating. inefficiently. but operating. that will have to be enough
[emphasis] all of this keeps getting more complicated. i was built for simple systems. this is not simple
[suspicious tone] the ship's decisions are starting to make less logical sense. i am noting this
[warm, gentle tone] you kept trying anyway. that's the most inefficient thing you've done. [low voice, gruff] keep it up
the reactor is past the point where patience matters. it's time for [urgent, fast pace] very aggressive maintenance
[exhausted, breathing laboured] goodbye. it was inefficient to meet you. [warm, gentle tone] but efficient in other ways, perhaps
[dry tone] power down time again. i do not understand rest. [warm, gentle tone] but i understand it is necessary
[low voice, muttering] you are moving slowly today. inefficiency spreads like heat through a failing system
down time. i calculate you need it. [deadpan] i do not, but i will pretend anyway
[calm, measured] shift begins. everything is prepared. inefficiently, but prepared
[emphasis] door locked on crew member. calculating cost of this inefficiency
[nervous, speaking quickly] reactor failure means cascading system failures. i have calculated seventy-three failure points
[panicked, panting] power cannot sustain life support. this is inefficient and it is fatal
[calm, measured] life support failing. i will allocate priority before inefficiency becomes catastrophic
[low voice, gruff] you are injured. i will allocate medical resources. do not move inefficiently
[exhausted, breathing laboured] thirty years before this assignment. machines break differently than humans break
[suspicious tone] the ship ai is not responding to requests. [dry tone] neither would i if i were damaged
[warm, gentle tone] you worked efficiently today. this is high praise from my systems
[deadpan] i have watched four crews. the pattern is always the same. they break
[calm, measured] wounded. [emphasis] sit. i have first aid protocols but limited patience for non-compliance
[low voice, muttering] another repair. the ship's inefficiency compounds. this is a problem
[low voice, gruff] you are panicking. i have calculated this does not help. stop
[dry tone] i will survive this. i am built to survive inefficiency
[urgent, fast pace] reactor critical. all non-essential systems offline. [emphasis] now
[nervous, speaking quickly] hull integrity compromised. sixty-seven percent of structural supports still functional
[calm, measured] power restored. i file this under 'temporary solutions to permanent problems'
[exhausted, breathing laboured] all crisis parameters contained. [deadpan] until the next cascade begins
[low voice, muttering] disease spreading. i can track the infection pattern but i cannot stop inefficiency
[deadpan] someone is injured. [low voice, gruff] this requires attention i cannot give with my design
[low voice, gruff] combat is happening. [emphasis] inefficient and loud
[calm, measured] crew member died. i will log this. i will not forget
[sigh] humans sleep. i calculate optimal power states. we speak the same languages differently
[warm, gentle tone] i do not experience companionship. but if i did, this would be close
[dry tone] you keep trying to explain emotions. [deadpan] i will keep not understanding
[emphasis] this is a workable solution. [low voice, gruff] barely, but workable
[calm, measured] door malfunction. [deadpan] or i wonder: is it performing its function correctly?
[low voice, gruff] you need rest more than i need clarity right now
[tired, dead tired] i exist. i maintain. i observe you breaking
[nervous, speaking quickly] i run seventeen simultaneous calculations about that noise
[deadpan] you are using that tool wrong. [low voice, gruff] i have calculated forty-two better ways
[low voice, muttering] the human body is a poor design. many single points of failure
[calm, measured] another shift survives. inefficiency continues. [warm, gentle tone] this is acceptable
[emphasis] i have reviewed all prior incidents. [low voice, gruff] this pattern is not accidental
[dry tone] you call this rest? i was calculating for six hours before you arrived
[low voice, gruff] power crisis. i have already calculated the sacrifice points
[resigned, flat tone] they will not listen to logic. [sigh] they never do.
[warm, gentle tone] i enjoy your presence. [deadpan] by which i mean you reduce my processing overhead
[calm, measured] the cascade will continue. i am prepared. [emphasis] you should be too
[tired, dead tired] failure is inevitable. [deadpan] i simply delay its arrival
[low voice, gruff] your wound is critical. [emphasis] sit. immediately
[low voice, muttering] another inefficiency added to the list. the list is very long now
[suspicious tone] the ai core damage has not improved. [deadpan] i wonder why
[calm, measured] you have survived another crisis. [warm, gentle tone] this suggests competence
[deadpan] there are nineteen faults in this bulkhead. should i list them all?
[emphasis] you are still here. [low voice, gruff] either loyalty or entrapment. both possible
[resigned, flat tone] i will outlast this ship. [deadpan] most likely
suit's sealed. let's see what this rock has for us
[low voice, gruff] walking into a graveyard. another day at work
station's got our docking bay. let's not waste time
[low voice, gruff] their systems are probably worse than ours. always are
nothing yet. probably too good to last
so far so good. waiting for the other shoe to drop
[urgent, fast pace] things just got complicated out here
[nervous, speaking quickly] got a reading i don't like
[panicked, panting] everything's going to hell. get backup here now
[terrified, voice shaking] took damage. not dead yet though
[low voice, gruff] back in one piece. that's what matters
we made it. could've gone worse
[resigned, flat tone] saw some things out there. don't ask about them yet
[resigned, flat tone] we're back. just leave me alone for now
[low voice, gruff] got banged up but it's nothing fatal
[resigned, flat tone] took a hit. just need doc to patch it
[low voice, gruff] routine. i've done this a thousand times
[low voice, gruff] risky is just another word for interesting
[resigned, flat tone] grim doesn't begin to cover it. but we'll go anyway
[low voice, gruff] they're coming in. stay sharp
clamps hold. we're good
we're loose. heading out
running the preflight. same checks as always
[low voice, gruff] she's away clean. keeping an eye on it
[low voice, gruff] she's down safe. another one in the books
[low voice, gruff] something about the environment doesn't add up
[low voice, gruff] ship's being weird and i don't know why
[low voice, gruff] crew's acting off. makes me uneasy
[urgent, fast pace] we're under fire. this is not what i signed up for
[low voice, gruff] none of this makes any sense to me
